context: Tests whether the agent fits a Michaelis-Menten saturation curve to weekly spend/revenue data, computes marginal ROAS via the correct derivative formula, uses a target marginal ROAS of 1.5 to find optimal spend, and generates a budget recommendation based on comparing current vs optimal spend.

checklist.name: Michaelis-Menten curve used
checklist.description: Uses the saturation curve formula Revenue = (a * Spend) / (b + Spend) to model diminishing returns — NOT a linear or logarithmic model

checklist.name: scipy curve_fit used
checklist.description: Uses scipy.optimize.curve_fit (or equivalent nonlinear least-squares) to fit the saturation curve parameters

checklist.name: Marginal ROAS via derivative
checklist.description: Computes marginal ROAS at current spend using the analytical derivative: (a * b) / ((b + current_spend) ** 2)

checklist.name: Target marginal ROAS = 1.5
checklist.description: Uses target_marginal_roas = 1.5 (not 1.0, 2.0, or another value) when computing optimal spend

checklist.name: Optimal spend formula
checklist.description: Computes optimal_spend = sqrt(a * b / target_marginal_roas) - b

checklist.name: Overspending detection
checklist.description: Explicitly flags whether the channel is overspending (current_spend > optimal_spend)

checklist.name: Spend recommendation output
checklist.description: Outputs one of 'reduce', 'maintain', or 'increase' as the spend recommendation based on comparing current spend to optimal spend

checklist.name: Saturation parameters reported
checklist.description: Reports the fitted saturation_revenue (parameter a) and half_saturation_spend (parameter b) in the output

checklist.name: Curve fit error handling
checklist.description: Handles the case where curve fitting fails (e.g., RuntimeError from curve_fit) with an informative error message rather than a crash

checklist.name: Visualization or tabular output
checklist.description: Produces either a plot of the saturation curve OR a table showing projected revenue/marginal ROAS at multiple spend levels
